title: Radiological contamination medical check-in
summary: Focused extreme medical support for radiological contamination medical check-in in offline, delayed-help, or collapse conditions.
content: Radiological events require sheltering, removing outer contamination, gentle washing, instructions, and symptom records; panic evacuation can increase exposure. This is not medical advice; it is a safety and organization checklist until qualified help is available.
materials: clean water if available. Context: “Radiological contamination medical check-in”.
soap or sanitizer. Context: “Radiological contamination medical check-in”.
gloves or clean barrier. Context: “Radiological contamination medical check-in”.
paper and pen. Context: “Radiological contamination medical check-in”.
phone, radio, or messenger — for «Radiological contamination medical check-in»
labels for time and symptoms — for «Radiological contamination medical check-in»
steps: Get inside, stay inside, and tune to official instructions if possible.
Remove outer clothing carefully if contamination is suspected and bag it away from people.
Wash exposed skin gently with soap and water if available; do not scrub harshly.
Keep dust out of mouth, nose, wounds, and food.
Record location, time outside, wounds, vomiting, burns, and official instructions received.
warnings: This is not medical advice and it is not a doctor's advice. It is offline emergency organization guidance only. Use local emergency numbers, trained first aid, pharmacists, clinics, doctors, poison centers, and official public-health instructions whenever available. Do not perform surgery, invasive procedures, diagnosis, prescription changes, or medicine dosing from this guide. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Radiological contamination medical check-in”.

Escalate immediately for trouble breathing, heavy bleeding, unconsciousness, severe pain, chest pain, stroke signs, severe burns, pregnancy danger signs, infants in distress, poisoning, or rapid worsening. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Radiological contamination medical check-in”.
Do not delay evacuation or professional care to finish a checklist. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Radiological contamination medical check-in”.
Keep privacy, consent, dignity, and local law in mind even during collapse. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Radiological contamination medical check-in”.
common_mistakes: Trying to diagnose instead of recognizing danger signs while carrying out “Radiological contamination medical check-in”
Forgetting times, medicines, allergies, and changes while carrying out “Radiological contamination medical check-in”
Touching fluids or wounds without hand hygiene while carrying out “Radiological contamination medical check-in”
Moving an injured person without a safety reason while carrying out “Radiological contamination medical check-in”
Letting panic, crowds, or rumors control care while carrying out “Radiological contamination medical check-in”
alternatives: Use certified first-aid training where available as a fallback for “Radiological contamination medical check-in”
Use official emergency services or the nearest clinic as a fallback for “Radiological contamination medical check-in”
Use a pharmacist for medicine questions as a fallback for “Radiological contamination medical check-in”
Use radio/phone consultation if transport is delayed as a fallback for “Radiological contamination medical check-in”
Use written handover notes when devices fail as a fallback for “Radiological contamination medical check-in”
failure_signs: Breathing, bleeding, consciousness, pain, fever, confusion, weakness, or skin color gets worse during “Radiological contamination medical check-in”
The helper cannot keep the scene clean or safe during “Radiological contamination medical check-in”
Transport, water, power, or communication failure blocks care during “Radiological contamination medical check-in”
The person becomes unable to drink, speak, walk, recognize people, or stay awake during “Radiological contamination medical check-in”
when_not_to_use: Do not use it to replace a doctor, ambulance, clinic, pharmacist, poison center, or trained first-aider when using the “Radiological contamination medical check-in” record
Do not use it for surgery or invasive treatment when using the “Radiological contamination medical check-in” record
Do not use it to change prescription medicines or doses when using the “Radiological contamination medical check-in” record
Do not force care on a conscious adult except immediate life-safety actions allowed by local rules when using the “Radiological contamination medical check-in” record
short_term: Reduce immediate danger, call or send for qualified help, keep the person clean, warm or cool as needed, and record facts without guessing. This note applies specifically to Radiological contamination medical check-in.
long_term: Build certified first-aid capacity, update medical records, protect supplies, practice handovers, and map routes to pharmacies, clinics, hospitals, and shelters. This note applies specifically to Radiological contamination medical check-in.
if_method_fails: Escalate by the fastest safe route: emergency number, clinic, hospital, pharmacist, poison center, radio medical consult, trained first-aider, or evacuation convoy. Record what failed and when. This note applies specifically to Radiological contamination medical check-in.
environment_notes: In apartments, shelters, rubble zones, heatwaves, floods, smoke, and outages, medical problems worsen because of crowding, poor air, poor hygiene, no elevator, no refrigeration, and delayed transport. This note applies specifically to Radiological contamination medical check-in.

title: Chemical plume symptom and exposure log
summary: Focused extreme medical support for chemical plume symptom and exposure log in offline, delayed-help, or collapse conditions.
content: After a chemical release, symptoms and exposure details help poison centers and doctors; guessing the chemical can waste time. This is not medical advice; it is a safety and organization checklist until qualified help is available.
materials: clean water if available. Context: “Chemical plume symptom and exposure log”.
soap or sanitizer. Context: “Chemical plume symptom and exposure log”.
gloves or clean barrier. Context: “Chemical plume symptom and exposure log”.
paper and pen. Context: “Chemical plume symptom and exposure log”.
phone, radio, or messenger — for «Chemical plume symptom and exposure log»
labels for time and symptoms — for «Chemical plume symptom and exposure log»
steps: Shelter-in-place or evacuate according to official instruction and wind direction if known.
Record odor, visible cloud, location, wind, time, symptoms, and contaminated clothing.
Separate exposed people from clean zones when safe.
Do not induce vomiting or give random antidotes.
Call poison/emergency services with the log.
warnings: This is not medical advice and it is not a doctor's advice. It is offline emergency organization guidance only. Use local emergency numbers, trained first aid, pharmacists, clinics, doctors, poison centers, and official public-health instructions whenever available. Do not perform surgery, invasive procedures, diagnosis, prescription changes, or medicine dosing from this guide. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Chemical plume symptom and exposure log”.
